questionText: You want to type a school report in Microsoft Word. What is the first thing you should do?
optionB: Open Microsoft Word and create a new document

questionText: You have finished typing your document and want to keep it safe. What should you do?
optionB: Press Ctrl+S to save your document

questionText: You typed a wrong word in your document. What is the easiest way to remove it?
optionB: Select the word and press Delete or Backspace
optionC: Print the document and cross it out

questionText: You want your document title to stand out from the rest of the text. What would you do?
optionB: Make the title text larger and bold

questionText: You notice a red wavy line under a word you typed. What does this mean?
optionC: The word may be spelled incorrectly
optionD: The font size is too small

questionText: You want to move a paragraph from the bottom of your document to the top. What is the best way to do this?
optionA: Retype it at the top and delete the original
optionB: Use Cut and Paste to move it
optionC: Print the document and rewrite it

questionText: You are typing a list of items and want each item to appear on its own line with a bullet point. Which feature should you use?
You want every page of your document to show your name at the top. Which feature should you use?
You want to change the font of your entire document to Arial. What should you do first?
optionC: Press Ctrl+A to select all text then change the font

questionText: You need to submit a 2-page report but accidentally typed everything on one page. What should you do?
optionB: Insert a Page Break to separate the content into two pages
optionC: Print and cut the page in half

questionText: You want to make your document look neat by adjusting the blank space around the edges of the page. Which setting should you change?
You copied text from a website and pasted it into Word but the formatting looks messy. What is the best thing to do?
optionC: Use Paste Special and choose Keep Text Only

questionText: You want to count how many words are in your essay. Where do you find this in Microsoft Word?
You are writing a story and want to add an image to make it more interesting. Where do you go to insert a picture?
You want to send your Microsoft Word document to a teacher who uses a different device. Which format should you save it in to make sure they can open it?
optionC: .pdf so anyone can open it without formatting issues

questionText: You accidentally deleted a whole paragraph. What is the quickest way to get it back?
optionC: Press Ctrl+Z to undo the action

questionText: You want all the body text in your document to be double-spaced for easy reading. Which setting controls this?
You finished your document and want to check for spelling mistakes before printing. What feature should you use?
optionC: Spell Check under the Review tab
